# Auto-PR Body Template

Used by `propose.py::render_pr_body()` when generating draft PRs from learned patterns.

## Template

```markdown
<!-- generated-by: memory-pattern-detector v1 -->
<!-- pattern-id: {pattern_id} -->

## Auto-Generated Learned Pattern

This PR was generated by the `memory-proposer` skill from {N} cases
with composite reward ≥ 0.6.

### Source Cases
- Case #{c1}
- Case #{c2}
- ...

**Average reward:** {avg_reward}

### Proposed Edit
Append to `skills/{target_skill}/SKILL.md` → `## Learned Patterns` section.

### Review Checklist
- [ ] Pattern is genuinely useful (not noise)
- [ ] Content is accurate and actionable
- [ ] No conflicts with existing skill guidance
- [ ] Source cases actually support the pattern

To reject: `gh pr close --delete-branch <this-pr>`.
```

## HTML Comments

Two HTML comments mark the PR as machine-generated:
- `<!-- generated-by: memory-pattern-detector v1 -->` — tool identifier
- `<!-- pattern-id: {pattern_id} -->` — ties the PR back to the patterns table

Tooling can grep for these to filter auto-PRs.

## Labels

- `automated` — catch-all for bot PRs
- `learned-pattern` — specific to memory-proposer

## Branch Naming

`bot/learned-pattern/{target_skill}-{content_hash_8_chars}`

Content hash makes branches idempotent: same content = same branch = no duplicate PR.

## Commit Message Format

```
chore(memory): add learned pattern to {target_skill}

Detected pattern {pattern_id} from {N} cases.
Avg reward: {avg_reward}

Generated-By: memory-pattern-detector
Pattern-Ids: {pattern_id}
Case-Ids: {c1},{c2},...
```

Git trailers (`Generated-By`, `Pattern-Ids`, `Case-Ids`) are grep-compatible.
